ocr.text: In the highly competitive landscape of the European retail and consumer goods sector, the latest same-store sales growth figures
for LVMH reveal significant insights into the performance of key categories. The table delineates the growth metrics for the
current fiscal period in comparison to the previous year, highlighting trends that are pivotal for strategic planning. Notably, the
"Fashion" category has demonstrated a robust growth trajectory, with a reported amount of 12.3%, categorized as "Positive."
This growth is indicative of the brand's successful strategies in engaging consumers and adapting to market demands. Similarly,
the "Jewelry" segment has also showcased a commendable performance with an amount of 7.8%, also classified as "Positive,"
reinforcing the brand's strength in high-end luxury offerings.
The data further includes a detailed breakdown of the "Watches" category, which remains an essential component of LVMH's
overall retail strategy. For reference, the "ComparisonPeriod" serves as a benchmark, allowing for a comprehensive evaluation
of the year-over-year changes. The previous year’s performance metrics, represented under the "PreviousYear" category,
provide critical context for understanding the current growth dynamics. As LVMH continues to navigate the complexities of the
